methods.description: Procedure for extracting, analyzing, and quantifying HBI lipids from samples using saponification, silica chromatography, and gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS).

steps.description: To enable quantification, an internal standard (9-octyl-8-hepta-decene; 0.02 μg) was added to each filter prior to extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: Add internal standard to sample filters for quantification

steps.description: Filters were then saponified (10% KOH; 90°C, 120 min; 10 mL), after which the non-polar fraction containing HBI lipids was collected using open column silica chromatography (ca. 1 g silica; 6-7 mL hexane; Belt et al., 2012).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform saponification and collect non-polar fraction using silica chromatography

steps.description: Analysis of HBIs was carried out using gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS) in selected ion monitoring (SIM, m/z 350 for IP 25 , 348 for HBIs IIa and IIb, and 346 for HBIs III and IV) mode using an Agilent 7890 series gas chromatograph (HP 5MS fused silica column; 30 m × 0.25 mm i.d., 0.25-μm film thickness) coupled to an Agilent 5975 mass spectrometric detector (Belt et al., 2012).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform GC-MS analysis in SIM mode for HBI biomarkers
outputs: GC-MS chromatograms and mass spectra data

steps.description: HBIs were identified by comparison of retention indices and mass spectra to those of authentic standards (Belt et al., 2000a;.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify HBI compounds using retention indices and mass spectra comparison to standards

steps.description: HBIs (pg mL -1 ) were quantified by comparing mass spectral intensities of molecular ions to that of the internal standard, and normalizing for differences in mass spectral fragmentation efficiency and volume filtered (ca. 30-1000, 900, 40-80 mL for sea ice, suspended and sinking particles, respectively).
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify HBI concentrations using internal standard comparison and normalization
inputs: Filtered volume (sample type dependent: sea ice, suspended particles, sinking particles)
